# methods

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

HTTP methods that Node.js core's HTTP parser supports.

This module provides an array of the HTTP methods supported by the Node.js HTTP parser. This is useful for various HTTP-related modules that need to know the complete set of valid HTTP methods.

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install methods
```

## API

```js
var methods = require('methods')
```

### methods

This module exports an array of all HTTP methods supported by the Node.js HTTP parser. The array consists of lowercase method names.

```js
console.log(methods)
// => ['get', 'post', 'put', 'head', 'delete', ...]
```

## Example

### Express.js Routing

The `methods` package is a key dependency for Express.js, enabling it to support various HTTP methods for routing. Express.js uses this package to create routing methods like `app.get()`, `app.post()`, etc.

```js
const express = require('express')
const app = express()

// GET method route for root path
app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Hello, World!\n')
})

// GET method route for /evening path
app.get('/evening', (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).type('text/plain').send('Good evening\n')
})

app.listen(3000, '127.0.0.1', () => {
  console.log('Server running at http://127.0.0.1:3000/')
})
```

### Creating a Router with Dynamic Methods

You can use the `methods` array to dynamically create route handlers for all HTTP methods:

```js
const methods = require('methods')
const router = {}

methods.forEach((method) => {
  router[method] = function(path, handler) {
    // Register route for this method and path
    console.log(`Registered ${method.toUpperCase()} handler for ${path}`)
  }
})

// Now you can use router.get(), router.post(), etc.
router.get('/', () => {})
router.post('/users', () => {})
```

## Supported Methods

The module provides all HTTP methods defined in RFC 7231 and RFC 5789, plus common additional methods used in HTTP applications:

- `get`
- `post`
- `put`
- `head`
- `delete`
- `options`
- `trace`
- `copy`
- `lock`
- `mkcol`
- `move`
- `purge`
- `propfind`
- `proppatch`
- `unlock`
- `report`
- `mkactivity`
- `checkout`
- `merge`
- `m-search`
- `notify`
- `subscribe`
- `unsubscribe`
- `patch`
- `search`
- `connect`
